title: Restore existing client selection + autofill in New Appointment dialog

requirements.summary: Reintroduce an existing-client selector in the New Appointment dialog (create mode only) to pick from the current user's client records.
acceptanceCriteria: When opening the AppointmentDialog in create mode (no existing appointment), a visible control exists to select an existing client from the current user's client records.
The client selection control is not shown (or is disabled) when editing an existing appointment, unless it previously existed in edit mode.

file_operations.description: Add a create-mode-only 'Client existant' selection control backed by the already-loaded clientRecords from useGetAllClientRecords; hide/disable this control when appointment is provided (edit mode) to match prior behavior.

requirements.summary: Autofill appointment fields from the selected existing client and keep the appointment tied to that client reference, while allowing clearing back to manual entry.
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting an existing client sets at minimum: referenceClient and nomClient in the form.
Selecting an existing client also populates other matching fields when available (e.g., phone number, address, service, notes) without preventing the user from editing appointment-specific fields.
The user can clear the selection to return to manual entry for a new/unlisted client.

file_operations.description: Track selected existing client in component state; on selection, populate formData.referenceClient + formData.nomClient and additional matching fields (phone/address/service/notes) from the chosen ClientRecord, and build clientRef from the selected record's owner+reference. Add a clear action that resets the selection and returns the form to manual entry (re-enabling reference editing as needed).

requirements.summary: Adjust create-mode duplicate-reference validation to allow selecting an existing client without triggering duplicate errors, while still blocking manual duplicates.
acceptanceCriteria: If the user selected an existing client from the restored selector, the form can be submitted even though referenceClient matches an existing client record.

If the user did not select an existing client and manually enters a referenceClient that matches an existing client record (case-insensitive), the form submission is blocked with the existing duplicate-reference error behavior.
No backend changes are required to support this behavior (client records are loaded via the existing useGetAllClientRecords hook).

file_operations.description: Update create-mode validation to conditionally bypass the duplicate-reference check when an existing client is selected; keep the current case-insensitive duplicate check when no existing client is selected (manual entry). Ensure submission uses the selected clientRef when present.
